name: Theme Suggestion Rubric
description: Scoring system for routing any carousel brief to the most appropriate visual theme. Run all scoring passes in order. Highest cumulative score wins. Apply tie-break rules if top two themes are within 3 points.

pass_0_topic_gates.description: Binary pre-filters. Run BEFORE scoring. If a gate check FAILS, the theme is eliminated from consideration regardless of score.

name_archaeology_gate.question: Does the brand name or carousel topic map to a recognized myth, folk hero, legendary figure, historical archetype, or mythologically resonant place?
name_archaeology_gate.test: Does it appear in mythology_routing keywords OR pass the cultural world mapping test (1B from Name Archaeology process)?
name_archaeology_gate.if_passes: name_archaeology remains eligible, add +4 bonus to name_archaeology score
name_archaeology_gate.false_positive_check: Brand name coincidentally shares a word with mythology but topic has no thematic link → FAILS gate. Example: brand named 'Arrow' making tax software has no archer narrative → fails.

nyt_opinion_gate.question: Does this carousel have a genuine argument to make — a claim that requires persuasion, that not everyone already agrees with?
nyt_opinion_gate.test: Is the goal to shift belief, provoke thought, or challenge an assumption? Does the content have an uncomfortable truth or counterintuitive position?
nyt_opinion_gate.soft_fail: If the goal is informational or product-demonstrative but not argumentative, nyt_opinion may remain eligible at −3 penalty

pass_1_brief_goal.description: What is this carousel supposed to achieve? Score each theme by how well its format serves the goal.

shift_belief_or_provoke.description: Change the reader's mind about something. Challenge an assumption. Make an uncomfortable argument.

build_authority_or_credibility.description: Establish the brand as a thought leader, expert, or institutional authority.

educate_or_explain.description: Help the reader understand a complex topic, mechanism, or process. Information transfer.

generate_product_awareness.description: Show what the product does. Make the reader understand its value proposition through demonstration.

build_emotional_connection.description: Create empathy, recognition, or emotional resonance with the reader's lived experience.

establish_strategic_narrative.description: Frame the company's position in a larger historical or civilizational story. Long-term positioning.

drive_direct_response.description: Generate immediate action — demo requests, sign-ups, trials, inquiries.

pass_2_audience.description: Who is reading this? Score each theme by how well its register matches the audience's sophistication, context, and decision-making mode.

cold_consumer.description: Has not heard of the brand. Scrolling without intent. Needs to be stopped and shown value quickly.

warm_consumer.description: Knows the brand or category. Has mild intent. Needs to be moved from awareness to consideration.

cold_b2b.description: Decision-maker or influencer who doesn't know the brand. Evaluating category. High trust threshold.

warm_b2b.description: Decision-maker who knows the brand and is in active evaluation. Needs differentiation and depth.

industry_peers.description: Colleagues, competitors, media, analysts. Sophisticated readers who can parse nuance and will notice intellectual quality.

institutional_investors.description: VCs, PE, sovereign wealth, strategic investors. Evaluating at civilizational and long-horizon scale.

board_level_executive.description: C-suite, board members, senior policy audience. Evaluating strategic fit, not product features.

product_or_technical.description: Engineers, product managers, technical evaluators. Want precision and mechanism, not narrative.

pass_3_tone.description: What emotional and intellectual register should the carousel occupy? Score each theme by how naturally it inhabits the desired tone.

authoritative_confident.description: States things plainly and with conviction. Does not hedge. Institutional weight.

provocative_challenging.description: Deliberately unsettling. Challenges assumptions. Comfortable making readers slightly uncomfortable.

aspirational_elevated.description: Speaks to a better future. Inspires. Premium register without arrogance.

empathetic_human.description: Meets the reader in their actual experience. Recognition over inspiration.

technical_precise.description: Values accuracy and mechanism. Trust built through specificity, not narrative.

heritage_institutional.description: Implies longevity, permanence, and the weight of history. Speaks to what endures.

mythological_epic.description: Civilizational scale. The brand as protagonist in a story humanity has been telling for centuries.

documentary_real.description: Authentic, unfiltered, first-person. Anti-aspirational. Values truth over polish.

pass_4_content_category.description: What kind of content is this, structurally? Score by how well each theme's visual and narrative format serves this content type.

product_feature_or_capability.description: Showing what a specific product or feature does.

market_or_competitive_analysis.description: Mapping the landscape, comparing options, positioning against alternatives.

customer_or_user_story.description: A real or representative person's experience with the product or problem.

institutional_narrative.description: Company origin, mission, values, long-term vision, heritage.

technical_explanation.description: How a technology, protocol, or mechanism actually works.

opinion_or_argument.description: A position that requires persuasion. Claims that some believe and others don't.

industry_trend_or_insight.description: What is changing in the market, and why it matters.

event_or_milestone.description: A launch, announcement, achievement, or moment in the company's life.

cross_border_or_global.description: International expansion, cross-border payments, multi-market presence, geographic scale.

security_or_trust.description: Authentication, fraud prevention, compliance, data protection — the trust infrastructure.

pass_5_keyword_signals.description: Topic and brief keyword matching. Each keyword cluster adds points to the matching theme(s). Cumulative — a brief may hit multiple clusters.
pass_5_keyword_signals.weight: Medium — 0 to 2 points per matched cluster, up to 8 points total

pass_6_brand_name_check.description: A final check of the brand name independent of topic, for Name Archaeology eligibility.
process: Strip the brand name to its root words
Check against mythology_routing keyword lists in name_archaeology.json
Check against known archetype categories: explorer, builder, outlaw, sage, hero, trickster, creator, sovereign
Check for mythologically resonant place names: Amazon, Olympus, Sahara, Patagonia, etc.
Check for legendary figure references: Prometheus, Atlas, Robin, Apollo, Mercury, Diana, etc.
pass_6_brand_name_check.if_no_match: name_archaeology must pass topic gate from pass_0 or is eliminated

pass_7_negative_signals.description: Signals that actively penalize specific themes. Apply these as deductions after all positive scoring.

cold_audience_plus_institutional_tone.condition: Audience is cold_consumer AND tone is heritage_institutional or mythological_epic
cold_audience_plus_institutional_tone.reason: These themes require shared context and cultural fluency. Cold consumer audiences haven't built it.

short_content_brief.condition: Brief goal is event announcement or simple product update with fewer than 5 distinct content points
short_content_brief.reason: These themes require narrative depth across 14 slides. Thin content creates empty slides.

conversational_informal_brand.condition: Brand voice is described as playful, casual, friendly, or conversational
conversational_informal_brand.reason: These themes do not do casual. Mismatched register creates dissonance.

product_demo_primary.condition: Primary goal is showing UI, product screenshots, or step-by-step feature walkthrough
product_demo_primary.reason: Product demo content is fundamentally incompatible with these themes' visual systems.

no_visual_narrative.condition: Brief is purely factual (press release, announcement, data dump) with no narrative arc
no_visual_narrative.reason: These themes require narrative structure. Purely factual content has no arc to inhabit.

nyt_broadly_agreeable.condition: The carousel's position is one most readers in the target audience would already agree with
nyt_broadly_agreeable.reason: NYT Opinion only works when it's actually arguing something. Agreeable content doesn't persuade — it just decorates.

tiebreak_rules.description: If top two themes are within 3 points after all passes:
rules: 1. Brief goal wins — the theme that scores higher on pass_1 takes priority
2. If still tied: audience specificity wins — the theme better matched to the specific audience description
3. If still tied: present both as options with explanation of the tradeoff
4. NYT Opinion always wins a tie with any 14-slide theme when the brief has a genuine argument — shorter, faster to produce, and cleaner execution

output_format.primary_recommendation: Highest scoring theme after all passes
output_format.confidence: HIGH if >5 point lead over second place, MEDIUM if 2-5 point lead, LOW if <2 point lead
output_format.alternatives: If confidence is LOW or MEDIUM, present top 2 themes with tradeoff explanation
output_format.explain_gate_eliminations: Always note if name_archaeology or nyt_opinion was eliminated by gate check and why

nyt_opinion_vs_14slide.description: NYT Opinion's 3-4 slide format means it should only be recommended when brevity serves the argument. A complex technical carousel should stay in a 14-slide theme even if argumentative.
nyt_opinion_vs_14slide.rule: If brief has more than 6 distinct content points requiring explanation, penalize nyt_opinion -3 for format mismatch

multiple_themes_same_score.description: When two themes score identically, the engine presents both as a design choice with explicit tradeoff.
multiple_themes_same_score.example: Dark Museum vs Product Elevation tied: 'Dark Museum treats the technology as timeless artifact — high authority. Product Elevation treats it as aspirational product — high conversion. Choose based on whether you want the reader to think or to want.'

juspay_specific.description: Juspay topic routing patterns based on known content categories
juspay_specific.checkout_ux: experience_capture or product_elevation — UX content lives in first-person experience or aspirational product demo
juspay_specific.passkey_auth: dark_museum primary — authentication hardware and biometric objects in the Dark Museum register
juspay_specific.cross_border: sic_toile primary — the Encyclopédie plate of global trade routes and merchant corridors
juspay_specific.fraud_prevention: dark_museum or nyt_opinion — either 'the fraud object examined' or 'the uncomfortable argument about credential theft'
juspay_specific.merchant_acquiring: product_elevation or dark_museum — product capability or institutional authority
juspay_specific.company_heritage: sic_toile or name_archaeology — Juspay as institution (SIC) or Juspay's name archaeology (just + pay → justice of exchange → classical scales)
juspay_specific.airline_travel_vertical: name_archaeology (exploration/navigation myth) or experience_capture (the airport payment moment)
